description: Alerting rules for the Azure IAM Privilege Escalation Chain scenario (Entra ID, Activity Log)

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure IAM PrivEsc Chain — Entra Risky Sign-In
relatedDashboards: Azure IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
rules.investigationGuide:
# Entra ID High-Risk Sign-In

A sign-in was flagged with high risk by Entra ID.

## Triage
1. Review actor and context: `FROM logs-azure.entra_id-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND azure.entra_id.riskLevel == "high" | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, source.ip, user_agent.original, azure.entra_id.riskLevel | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. Unfamiliar IP, device, or location is higher signal
3. Cross-check Activity Logs for Entra ID / RBAC changes after the sign-in

## Escalate when
- Affected principal is privileged or has production access
- Service account or break-glass user
- Source IP outside corp/VPN

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure IAM PrivEsc Chain — Owner Role Assignment
relatedDashboards: Azure IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
rules.investigationGuide:
# Owner Role Assignment

An Owner role assignment was written — very high signal.

## Triage
1. Confirm: `FROM logs-azure.activitylogs-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action == "Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write" AND azure.activitylogs.properties LIKE "*Owner*" | KEEP @timestamp, user.name, azure.activitylogs.properties, source.ip | SORT @timestamp DESC`
2. Revoke the assignment if unauthorized
3. Check caller's full Activity Logs timeline

## Escalate when
- **Always page security on-call.** Owner role assignment by an unexpected principal is rare and high-confidence.

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure IAM PrivEsc Chain — Subscription Enumeration
relatedDashboards: Azure IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
rules.investigationGuide:
# Subscription Enumeration

Repeated or suspicious subscription read activity from Activity Logs.

## Triage
1. Scope callers: `FROM logs-azure.activitylogs-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND event.action LIKE "*subscriptions/read*" | STATS reads = COUNT(*) BY user.name, source.ip | SORT reads DESC | LIMIT 25`
2. Map follow-on writes to Entra ID / RBAC or resource changes
3. Lock down or revoke sessions if the pattern is anomalous

## Escalate when
- Enumeration precedes role assignment or Owner grants
- Same caller from unusual geo or IP

rules.name: [CloudLoadGen] Azure IAM PrivEsc Chain — High Volume Entra Risk and IAM Activity Log Events
relatedDashboards: Azure IAM Privilege Escalation Chain — overview
rules.investigationGuide:
# High Volume of Dangerous Entra ID / RBAC Actions

**Apex rule — treat as confirmed privilege escalation.**

## Triage
1. Identify principal: `FROM logs-azure.entra_id-*, logs-azure.activitylogs-* | WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1h AND (azure.entra_id.riskLevel == "high" OR event.action IN ("Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write", "Microsoft.Authorization/roleDefinitions/write", "Microsoft.Authorization/elevateAccess/action")) | STATS dangerous = COUNT(*) BY user.name, event.action | SORT dangerous DESC`
2. Reconstruct full timeline on the linked dashboard
3. Disable the principal in Entra ID, invalidate sessions, revoke risky assignments

## Escalate when
- **Always.** This is the apex rule of the IAM PrivEsc chain.
